reasoning: The stock has faced severe downward pressure recently, dropping 28% in February due to extremely poor 2026 guidance, and further declining over the last week likely due to being removed from two major equity indices in March. The rebalance selling typically peaks around the index change date (around March 20 for Russell/S&P). Post-rebalance, the forced selling pressure from index funds usually abates, creating an environment where the stock could stabilize or bounce slightly due to short covering. The upcoming Q1 earnings catalyst is not until late April (April 22), so no expected high-volatility news should hit in the next week. Given the standard stock market base rate for a 1-week positive return (around 52-53%), the fact that it is oversold, and the mixed statistical indicators (linear model P=0.48 vs combined historical P=0.57), I lean slightly towards a typical weekly positive drift at 53%.
